element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary objective throughout all four worlds
Core mechanic - freeze enemies into ice platforms or defeat them
Defensive/puzzle solving - create barriers in front of Kickle
Spatial puzzle solving - coordinate freeze placement with pathfinding
Progressive difficulty - different enemy behaviors introduced gradually
Culmination of world mechanics - extended combat sequences
Post-game content with increased complexity and enemy density

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation within worlds with punctuated difficulty spikes at boss encounters; Special Game provides continuous high-tension climax

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (freezing, pillar creation) in safe contexts; single enemy types; straightforward pathing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Multiple enemy types per level; water navigation puzzles requiring freeze-bridge coordination; increased enemy density in Japanese version
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters at world conclusions; complex spatial puzzles; heightened time pressure from enemy movement patterns
notable_spikes: World 1 Boss - first major combat test
World 2-3 - Japanese version significantly harder than international versions by approximately one full world difficulty tier
Special Game entry - 30-level gauntlet of maximum difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels requiring players to freeze enemies into ice platforms to create walkways across water barriers; demands spatial prediction and freeze timing
Progression from single-enemy encounters toward multi-enemy levels; Japanese version includes higher enemy density throughout
Large-scale encounters at world conclusions; require sustained freezing and avoidance mechanics; bosses require multiple hits to defeat (more in Japanese version)
Limited maneuvering room combined with enemy positioning creates tactical puzzle-solving scenarios

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Kickle Cubicle entry)
